scenes.text: This is a real sixty-second Cockroach Crawler demo: install the package, run a deterministic crawl, verify the denial, and inspect the source record.
Install one package. It exposes the command line, JavaScript library, and strict agent adapter with no hosted account or signup.
The captured command authorizes the offline fixture and fixes page, depth, request, per-page byte, total-byte, and delay ceilings before execution.
The real result contains two structured pages, status, depth, and content hashes. Removing the required network authority blocks dispatch and returns zero pages.
The source adapter converts that crawl into a normalized evidence record with source identity, adapter version, provenance, metadata, warnings, and a separate record hash.
Use it for documentation retrieval, content quality checks, and bounded agent research. Install locally, then review every limit at cockroachcrawler dot com.
One package, two deliberate surfaces: a hardened local crawler and a restricted serverless endpoint.
Doctor reports web and GitHub ready, YouTube partial, and X and Reddit as credentialed APIs.
There is no cookie scraping or credential fallback. Provider status stays explicit.
Worker requests need an HTTPS allowlist and secret. Redirect escape is blocked.
CLI for control. Serverless for small jobs. Both return records.
This proof starts a deterministic server on loopback. It uses no internet connection, API key, account, or external side effect.
The real command explicitly authorizes loopback and fixes page, depth, request, per-page byte, total-byte, and delay budgets.
The command returns two structured pages with status, depth, and SHA-256 content hashes. The same seed without private-network authority is denied.
Source doctor comes from the same credential-free runtime: web and public GitHub ready, YouTube partial, and X and Reddit requiring credentials.
The web adapter converts crawler output into normalized records with source, type, adapter version, provenance, metadata, and a separate record hash.
